title: Train Model
description: Train a YOLO model with your annotated dataset

env.description: Annotix needs Python with ultralytics to train models. Click below to set up automatically.
env.setupHint: This may take a few minutes on first setup

presets.title: Scenario Preset
presets.small_objectsDesc: Few classes, small objects, fast video. Optimized ROI with freeze.
presets.industrialDesc: Fixed camera inspection, defect detection. High precision, minimal augmentation.
presets.trafficDesc: Vehicles & pedestrians, many objects, scale variation. Dense scenes.
presets.edge_mobileDesc: Raspberry Pi, phones, IoT. Minimal size, maximum speed.
presets.medicalDesc: Lesions, cells, high precision. Conservative LR, heavy freeze.
presets.aerialDesc: Drone/satellite imagery, tiny objects, any orientation. High resolution.

waiting.pro: First complete epoch hasn't finished yet. Once it does, we'll read train_loss, val_loss and the target metric to begin diagnosing.

warmingUp.pro: {{n}} of 3 minimum epochs. With so few points any trend is noise: at least 3 samples are needed to estimate loss and metric slopes. Holding off on judgment.
warmingUp.fun: Warming up 🏃 ({{n}}/3), too early to judge

healthy.pro: Healthy training: train_loss is decreasing and the target metric improves ≈{{rate}}%/epoch over the recent window. This is the expected scenario; if it holds until the schedule ends, nothing to tweak.

excellent.pro: {{metric}} = {{value}}% — very high. Before celebrating, validate on an independent test set to rule out overfitting or train/val data leakage. If confirmed, this checkpoint is ready to export.
excellent.fun: Crushing it, {{metric}} {{value}}%! Validate on test too ⚡

strong.pro: {{metric}} = {{value}}% is a competitive level. There's usually additional margin by dropping the learning rate (lr0 × 0.1) in final epochs, or extending training with strong augmentation for better generalization.
strong.fun: Looking sharp ({{metric}} {{value}}%). A bit of polish ✨

overfitting.pro: Overfitting signal: train_loss still drops but val_loss has started to rise. Means the model is memorizing train instead of generalizing. Still mild — if it persists 5-6 more epochs, raise weight_decay, strengthen augmentation (mosaic, mixup, hsv), or enable early stop.
overfitting.fun: Starting to memorize 📚. If it keeps up, more aug.

overfitSevere.pro: Sustained severe overfitting: the val_loss − train_loss gap grew to {{gap}} and keeps widening over several epochs. The model no longer generalizes. Restore the best previous checkpoint and relaunch with more regularization (weight_decay, dropout, augmentation) or more data.
overfitSevere.fun: Serious overfit 🚨 gap {{gap}} and growing. Back to best.

valLossUp.pro: val_loss rising without matching progress in train_loss. Different from classic overfitting: here the model isn't learning train well either. Typical suspects: LR too high for this stage, noisy data, or batch-norm conflicting with a small batch.
valLossUp.fun: Val angry for no reason 😤 check LR and data.

lrTooHigh.pro: train_loss oscillates with coefficient of variation {{cv}}% and multiple sign changes, failing to descend. That's the classic too-high LR pattern: steps overshoot the minimum. Try halving lr0 or enabling cos_lr for smooth decay.
lrTooHigh.fun: Loss bouncing (CV {{cv}}%). LR too high, drop it.

lrTooLow.pro: After several epochs, the metric barely moves and loss decreases very slowly. Probable cause: lr0 too small, or model underpowered for the task. Raise lr0 2-3×, try a larger model, or check pretrained=True.

boxDominates.pro: box_loss averages {{ratio}}× cls_loss in the recent window. Training is focusing hard on locating boxes and little on classifying. If classification matters as much as detection, raise the 'cls' weight in loss config.
boxDominates.fun: box eats cls ({{ratio}}×). Bump cls weight.

clsDominates.pro: cls_loss averages {{ratio}}× box_loss. The model classifies well but localizes poorly, or there's strong class imbalance. Check class distribution and consider raising the 'box' weight.

dflHigh.pro: dfl_loss high vs box_loss means the box-coordinate distribution isn't converging: imprecise boxes. Raise the 'dfl' weight or improve annotation quality/consistency.

regression.pro: {{metric}} dropped from {{best}} to {{now}} over {{epochs}} consecutive epochs. This is no longer punctual noise: the model has steadily gotten worse. Worth comparing against the best checkpoint and, if the schedule has no recovery mechanism, stopping and reverting to best.
regression.fun: Backing up for a while: {{metric}} {{best}}→{{now}} in {{epochs}} epochs 📉

earlyStop.pro: Best value hasn't been beaten in {{epochs}} epochs. No guarantee it won't improve later (LR schedules can revive the model), but statistically the ROI is decaying — reasonable to stop and keep the best if compute time matters.
earlyStop.fun: {{epochs}} epochs with no new best. Stopping is fair 🛑

plateau.pro: Target metric barely moving in recent epochs. Could be a real stall or a plateau before another jump when LR decays. If cos_lr isn't active, manually dropping lr0 by ×0.1 often breaks plateaus.

plateauLow.pro: Stuck at {{value}}%, a low level to settle on. Typical causes: dataset too small or imbalanced, noisy labels, or model inadequate for the problem's complexity. Inspect the data before tuning hyperparameters.

converged.pro: Converged: {{metric}} = {{value}}% stable for several epochs. The model has squeezed what it could from this schedule. Extra epochs won't help; export and validate.

lossExploding.pro: train_loss spiked ×{{ratio}} ({{from}} → {{to}}). That's divergence: the optimizer went off the rails. Common causes: lr0 too high, AMP with bad scaling, or gradients without clipping. Lowering lr0 or disabling AMP usually fixes it.

nan.pro: NaN/Inf appeared in the loss. No recovery from that: weights are corrupted. Common causes: lr0 too high, AMP instability, corrupt images, or invalid annotations (boxes out of range, etc.). Must restart.

deadModel.pro: With {{metric}} = {{value}}% after {{epochs}} epochs, the model isn't learning. It's not a tuning issue — check the basics: properly formatted labels, pretrained=True, reasonable LR, and that the dataset actually contains what you want to detect.

precisionHigh.pro: Precision {{precision}}% with recall {{recall}}%: the model is conservative, only predicting when very confident and missing many positives. Useful if avoiding false positives is the priority; otherwise, lower the confidence threshold or raise the cls weight.

recallHigh.pro: Recall {{recall}}% with precision {{precision}}%: the model over-predicts with many false positives. Useful if missing positives is unacceptable; otherwise, raise the confidence threshold or tune NMS IoU.

valTooEasy.pro: train_loss ({{train}}) consistently above val_loss ({{val}}) is unusual. Usually indicates data leakage (same images in train and val) or a validation split easier than the training split. Inspect how the split was built.

noisy.pro: train_loss is decreasing but with high noise (CV={{cv}}%). Happens with small batches or very heterogeneous data: each batch gives a different signal. Increasing batch size, enabling AMP, or raising nbs stabilizes training.

warmupPhase.pro: Warmup phase active: LR is still rising from a low value toward lr0. Metrics in this stretch don't yet reflect real training behavior — wait for LR to stabilize.

lrDecayed.pro: Current LR is tiny compared to the peak: the scheduler has used up its decay range. At this stage the model barely changes. If there's no recent improvement, it won't come without resuming with a new cycle.
lrDecayed.fun: LR near zero. No juice to improve.

neutral.pro: No conclusive patterns in the recent window: no strong overfitting, no divergence, no convergence either. Keep training and observe.

result.description: Your model has been trained successfully.

backend.title: Training Backend
backend.description: Choose the framework for training your model
backend.installingPackagesDesc: This may take a few minutes. Your configuration will be preserved.

execution.title: Execution Mode
execution.description: Choose how to run the training
execution.localDesc: Run training locally using your GPU. Requires installed packages.
execution.downloadDesc: Download a ZIP with dataset, script and notebook to train elsewhere.
execution.cloudDesc: Train on cloud GPUs using Kaggle, Vertex AI, Colab Enterprise, Lightning AI, Hugging Face or Saturn Cloud.
execution.browserAutomationDesc: Free training via Google Colab using browser automation

params.lr_encoderDesc: Learning rate for the encoder backbone
params.grad_accum_stepsDesc: Number of steps to accumulate gradients
params.early_stoppingDesc: Stop training when validation loss plateaus
params.gradient_checkpointingDesc: Trade compute for memory — enables larger models
params.warmup_itersDesc: Number of warmup iterations before full LR
params.batchSizeDesc: -1 for auto (60% GPU memory). Larger = more stable gradients.
params.epochsDesc: Total passes over dataset. More = better convergence but slower.
params.imgszDesc: Input resolution. Quadratic impact on speed. Match to object size.
params.patienceDesc: Epochs without improvement before early stopping.
params.max_detDesc: Maximum detections per image. Lower = faster post-processing.
params.multi_scaleDesc: Random size variation per batch. Improves scale robustness.
params.rectDesc: Minimal padding respecting aspect ratio. More efficient for non-square images.
params.cacheDesc: Cache images in RAM (fast) or disk. Speeds up training significantly.
params.optimizerDesc: Auto selects the best optimizer for your YOLO version.
params.lr0Desc: Initial learning rate. Higher = faster but can be unstable.
params.lrfDesc: Final LR as fraction of initial: lr_final = lr0 × lrf.
params.cos_lrDesc: Cosine scheduler: smooth decay, better fine convergence than linear.
params.warmup_epochsDesc: Epochs where LR gradually ramps up from near-zero to lr0.
params.momentumDesc: SGD momentum or Adam beta1. Higher = smoother training.
params.weight_decayDesc: L2 regularization. Higher = stronger regularization against overfitting.
params.boxDesc: Localization loss weight. Increase for precise bounding boxes.
params.clsDesc: Classification loss weight. Increase with few classes.
params.dflDesc: Distribution Focal Loss. Set to 0 for YOLO26.
params.pretrainedDesc: Use COCO pretrained weights. Always recommended unless dataset is very large.
params.freezeDesc: Freeze first N layers (backbone). 0 = train all. Higher with small datasets.
params.ampDesc: FP16/FP32 mixed precision. Faster and less memory. Disable only if NaN errors.
params.nbsDesc: Nominal batch size for loss normalization. Rarely needs adjustment.
params.single_clsDesc: Treat all classes as one. For binary detection (object/no object).
params.warmup_bias_lrDesc: Learning rate for bias parameters during warmup.
params.valSplitDesc: Fraction of images for validation. 0.2 = 80% train, 20% val.
params.workersDesc: Data loading threads. More = faster loading but more CPU/RAM.
params.deviceDesc: Compute device. Auto detects the best available.
params.mosaicDesc: Combines 4 images. Great for dense scenes. Reduce with small objects.
params.close_mosaicDesc: Disable mosaic for last N epochs. Stabilizes final training phase.
params.mixupDesc: Blends two images with transparency. Light regularization.
params.translateDesc: Random horizontal/vertical shift as fraction of image size.
params.scaleDesc: Random zoom variation. Increase if objects vary in apparent size.
params.fliplrDesc: Horizontal flip probability. Disable if laterality matters.
params.flipudDesc: Vertical flip probability. Enable for aerial/microscopy images.
params.degreesDesc: Random rotation range in degrees. 180 for aerial (any orientation).
params.shearDesc: Shear deformation in degrees. Use sparingly.
params.hsv_hDesc: Color hue variation. Keep low if colors are diagnostically relevant.
params.hsv_sDesc: Saturation variation. Reduce for controlled lighting.
params.hsv_vDesc: Brightness variation. Increase for variable lighting conditions.
params.erasingDesc: Random erasing simulates occlusion. Reduce with small objects.
params.freeze_encoderDesc: Freeze encoder weights for transfer learning
params.do_reduce_labelsDesc: Shift label IDs by 1 (some datasets use 255 for background)
params.warmup_ratioDesc: Fraction of training for linear warmup
params.angle_versionDesc: Angle encoding for oriented bounding boxes
params.window_sizeDesc: Window size for time series processing
params.max_prediction_lengthDesc: Maximum number of time steps to predict
params.max_encoder_lengthDesc: Maximum number of time steps for encoder input
params.gradient_clip_valDesc: Gradient clipping value to prevent exploding gradients
params.hidden_sizeDesc: Hidden layer size for the model
params.contaminationDesc: Expected fraction of anomalies in the dataset
params.n_estimatorsDesc: Number of estimators for ensemble methods
params.max_iterDesc: Maximum number of iterations for clustering
params.d_modelDesc: Dimension of the model's hidden representation

model.sizeDescNano: Fastest, lower accuracy. Good for edge devices.

fineTuneDesc: Continue training from a previously trained model

cloud.selectProviderDesc: Choose where to run your training job

providerDesc.vertex_ai_custom: Custom training with any GPU on GCP
providerDesc.lightning_ai: Free T4/L4 GPUs via Lightning Studios
providerDesc.saturn_cloud: Free T4 GPU instances for ML

cancelConfirmMessage: Unsaved progress will be lost. Best/last checkpoints stay on disk. Cancel?

weights.lastHint: last.pt available (last checkpoint, enables true resume)
